mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines signal monetary easing that reduces discount rates on equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. A 50+ basis point drop over 3 months triggers the mechanism and predicts positive 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines > 50 basis points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.33% suggests room for easing in crisis conditions
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following financial stress
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window post-trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment signals economic deterioration and reduced consumer spending power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns. Unemployment increases > 0.3 percentage points within 2 months activate the signal.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.7% is historically low; any sharp rise signals recession risk
regime_hint: early-stage labor market deterioration preceding recession
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales decline signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. Mechanism is always-on, using 3-month moving average of RSAFS.
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 683291M; housing stress may suppress consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX outperforms in > 50% of periods with declining RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline combined with rising unemployment signals dual demand shock: reduced construction investment and weakened consumer balance sheets. Joint deterioration in both series predicts negative 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 10% from 3-month average AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1446K suggests housing stress; credit crisis context
regime_hint: financial crisis with dual demand contraction
failure_condition: Interaction term (HOUST decline × UNRATE rise) shows no significant negative correlation with 20-day returns (p > 0.10), or mechanism fails to outperform single-series models by > 5% in Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production decline signals reduced capacity utilization and corporate profitability stress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns. Mechanism activates when 3-month INDPRO growth turns negative.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 104.55; financial crisis may suppress manufacturing
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment decline combined with rising unemployment signals accelerating labor market deterioration, triggering negative equity returns. Joint signal captures recession momentum better than single series.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100K month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.15pp simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 152744K; credit crisis context suggests employment risk
failure_condition: Interaction term shows no significant negative correlation with 20-day returns (p > 0.10), or joint signal underperforms individual series by > 3% in Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI combined with elevated federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations. When both CPIAUCSL growth > 2.5% annualized and FEDFUNDS > 2.0%, negative returns follow.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 295.62; FEDFUNDS at 2.33% suggests tightening bias
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Joint tightening signal shows no significant negative correlation with 60-day returns (p > 0.10), or mechanism fails to explain variance beyond single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly combined with monthly retail sales momentum predicts equity returns. Regime-conditional: in late-cycle expansions (GDPC1 growth slowing), RSAFS weakness predicts negative returns; in early recovery, RSAFS strength predicts positive returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 2% annualized (late-cycle regime) AND RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 19699.5B; credit crisis suggests late-cycle stress
failure_condition: Regime-conditional direction reversal does not occur as predicted (positive returns in early recovery, negative in late-cycle), or Rank IC < 0.06 in late-cycle regime over any 2-year window.
